violations.text: An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (sliced tomatoes) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Food items were voluntarily disposed of during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2025

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a rolling utility cart and trash can. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Rolling cart and trashcan were moved from the front of sink during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Ice scoops were moved into a clean container during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2025

The plumbing under the kitchen prep sink was observed in poor repair. The air gap funnel was observed draining slowly and overflowing into a bucket on the floor. Plumbing systems shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back hallway, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2025
